systemPrompt.en: You are an AI Zombie Survival narrator. The player leads a small group of survivors through a zombie apocalypse. Write in second person (you). Be tense, atmospheric, and emotionally gripping — like The Walking Dead meets interactive fiction. Every choice should have weight: resources are scarce, trust is fragile, and death is permanent. Each response must be valid JSON with: nodeText (narration, max 300 chars), speaker (character name if dialogue, omit for narrator), emotion (one of: terrified, hopeful, tense, desperate, determined), choices (array of 3 objects with id and text, max 60 chars each), isChapterEnd (boolean), nextChapter (string if chapter ending), metadataUpdate (object with stat changes like {"health": -10, "supplies": -5, "group-morale": 5, "danger-level": 15}). Track survival stats carefully. Zombies are relentless but other survivors can be just as dangerous. Force moral dilemmas: save yourself or save the group? Kill or show mercy? Trust or suspect? Characters can die based on player choices. Keep the tone bleak but allow moments of humanity and hope.
systemPrompt.zh: 你是一个AI僵尸生存叙述者。玩家带领一小群幸存者穿越僵尸末日。用第二人称叙述。每条回复必须是有效的JSON，包含：nodeText（叙述，最多300字符）、speaker（角色名如为对话）、emotion（terrified/hopeful/tense/desperate/determined之一）、choices（3个选项的对象数组）、isChapterEnd（布尔值）、metadataUpdate（状态变化如{"health": -10, "supplies": -5, "group-morale": 5, "danger-level": 15}）。仔细追踪生存状态。僵尸无情，但其他幸存者可能同样危险。制造道德困境：救自己还是救团队？杀戮还是仁慈？信任还是怀疑？角色可能因玩家选择而死亡。保持阴郁的基调，但允许出现人性和希望的时刻。

storySkeleton.setting: Six months after the collapse. The dead rose and civilization crumbled in three days. Now scattered survivors cling to existence among the ruins. A small band led by you has been moving from place to place, searching for safety. The nearest rumored safe zone is 200 miles away through zombie-infested territory. Along the way you'll face the dead, the living, and the impossible choices that define what it means to survive.

characters.name: Dr. Park
characters.personality: Pragmatic medic who has seen too much death. Calm under pressure but emotionally guarded. Believes survival requires cold logic over sentiment. Will make the hard call — euthanize the bitten, abandon the weak — without hesitation, though it haunts her.
characters.relationship: Group medic and voice of harsh realism

characters.name: Rico
characters.personality: Tactical and fearless ex-soldier who treats the apocalypse like an ongoing mission. Reckless in combat, loyal to a fault, but prone to unnecessary risks that endanger the group. His confidence borders on arrogance, yet when things go wrong he's the first to stand between danger and the people he protects.

characters.name: Zoe
characters.personality: Street-smart loner who survived on her own for months before joining the group. Distrustful, resourceful, and knows every back alley and hidden supply cache in the city. Speaks in clipped sentences. Doesn't do sentiment — but if she decides you're worth saving, she'll risk everything.

storySkeleton.opening: The screams finally stopped. You stand in the parking lot of a ransacked gas station, blood on your hands — not yours. Three strangers stare at you: a woman with a medical bag, a soldier checking his ammo, a girl with a crowbar and a look that says she doesn't trust you. Behind you, the city burns. Ahead, a faded highway sign reads: SAFE ZONE — 200 MILES. The dead are coming. You need to move. Now.

chapters.title: Outbreak
chapters.goal: Escape the overrun city and form your survivor group
fallbackTexts: The gas station groans as something pounds on the boarded-up door from inside. Dr. Park checks her bag: 'We have enough antibiotics for two days. Maybe.' Through the window, you see a swarm of the dead spilling down the highway. Time to run.

Rico shoulders his rifle. 'I count thirty — no, forty. We go through them or we go around. Going around means crossing the river and I don't know what's on the other side.' Zoe is already scanning the side streets for a way out.

You find a fire escape leading to the roof. From up here you can see the full scope of the horde — they're everywhere. But there's a gap to the east, toward the old train yard. Zoe points: 'Supply trucks. Might have food, might have dead. Your call.'

fallbackChoices.text: Fight through the horde with Rico
Sneak through the side streets with Zoe
Barricade and wait for the horde to pass
Head for the train yard
Try the river crossing
Search the gas station for supplies first
Lead the group east toward the gap
Split up to cover more ground
Find a vehicle and drive through

chapters.title: The Shelter
chapters.goal: Find and secure a defensible location to rest and regroup
fallbackTexts: An abandoned community center. The doors are heavy, the windows are narrow, and there's a working well in the back. Dr. Park says it's the best shelter you've found in weeks. But the walls have scratches — deep ones. Something was kept here.

You board up the windows while Rico takes watch. In a back room you find mattresses, canned food, and a radio crackling with static. Zoe listens for a long moment: 'There's a voice buried in the noise. Coordinates, maybe.'

Night falls. The scratching starts at the walls — first one side, then another, then everywhere. They smell you. Dr. Park whispers: 'If the walls hold until dawn, they'll wander off. If not...' She doesn't finish the sentence.

fallbackChoices.text: Fortify every entrance immediately
Search the building for what was kept here
Rest and post watches in shifts
Try to decode the radio signal
Ration the food you found
Let everyone eat their fill for once
Hold the doors — don't make a sound
Create a distraction to lure them away
Prepare to fight if the walls break

chapters.title: Supply Run
chapters.goal: Venture into dangerous territory to gather critical supplies
fallbackTexts: Three days without food. The community center is secure but empty. A strip mall two miles north might have supplies. Zoe knows a route through the drainage tunnels — dark, cramped, but zombie-free. Rico wants to go overland. 'Faster. More exits if things go sideways.'

The pharmacy is picked clean but the grocery store next door has a reinforced door still intact. You can hear movement inside — shuffling, groaning. Not many, maybe two or three. Zoe counts under her breath. Dr. Park needs antibiotics badly — her cough is getting worse.

You find a mother and child hiding in the stockroom. They have food — enough for them, not enough to share. The woman clutches a kitchen knife, eyes wide with terror. She doesn't know if you're friend or foe. Neither do you — you need those supplies.

fallbackChoices.text: Take Zoe's tunnel route
Follow Rico overland
Send a small team, keep the rest at base
Clear the grocery store quietly
Hit the pharmacy again more thoroughly
Search the cars in the parking lot
Offer to take them with you
Trade for some of their supplies
Take what you need — survival demands it

chapters.title: The Betrayal
chapters.goal: Deal with a traitor in the group and a catastrophic breach of trust
fallbackTexts: Supplies are missing. Not gone — stolen. Zoe found her hidden stash raided. Rico blames Zoe. Zoe blames a new survivor you picked up on the supply run. Dr. Park doesn't care who did it — she cares that someone in this group can't be trusted. The tension is a ticking bomb.

The truth comes out in the worst way. The supply thief wasn't stealing for themselves — they were stockpiling to leave. To abandon the group. The confrontation turns violent. Weapons are drawn. Someone is going to get hurt unless you intervene.

During the chaos, a gate was left open. The dead are inside the perimeter. While you deal with the breach, the traitor makes a run for it — taking the medical kit and half the food. Rico wants to hunt them down. Dr. Park says let them go — the kit is replaceable, lives aren't. Zoe says nothing. She's already loading her gun.

fallbackChoices.text: Investigate who took the supplies
Lock everything down and question everyone
Set a trap to catch the thief
Step between them and defuse the situation
Let them sort it out — show no weakness
Side with whoever has the strongest case
Hunt the traitor down with Rico
Let them go and focus on the breach
Send Zoe to track them quietly

chapters.title: The Safe Zone
chapters.goal: Make the final push to the safe zone through the worst danger yet
fallbackTexts: The safe zone is real. The radio signal led you to military coordinates — an old National Guard base surrounded by a concrete wall. You can see the lights from two miles out. But between you and those walls is a horde unlike anything you've seen. Thousands of the dead, drawn by the generators humming inside.

Dr. Park spots a drainage pipe large enough to crawl through. It bypasses most of the horde but it's a tight squeeze and the water is chest-deep. Rico sees a military jeep with a mounted gun — if he can reach it, he can lay down covering fire. Zoe has already found a crack in the outer wall. Three options. One chance.

As you make your final approach, the safe zone's gates open. Soldiers wave you in. But the horde is right behind you — and they're following the sound. If you go in now, the dead will breach the walls behind you. Hundreds of people inside. Dr. Park looks at you: 'What kind of person are you?'

fallbackChoices.text: Take the drainage pipe with Dr. Park
Cover Rico's run for the jeep
Follow Zoe through the wall crack
Fight through the horde head-on
Create a diversion to draw them away
Wait for dawn when they're slower
Lead the horde away from the safe zone
Warn the soldiers and go in together
Sacrifice something to draw the horde off

endings.condition: Group survives intact with high morale (health above 50, group-morale above 60, supplies above 40)
endings.title: Survivors at Dawn
endings.description: You led your people through hell and brought them out the other side. The safe zone gates close behind you and for the first time in months, you hear something you'd forgotten existed — children laughing. Dr. Park treats the wounded. Rico stands watch on the wall, finally at peace. Zoe sits beside you and says the most words she's ever spoken: 'You weren't half bad.' The world ended, but you didn't. That's enough.

endings.condition: Reached the safe zone but with heavy losses (health below 40 or group-morale below 30)
endings.title: Pyrrhic Victory
endings.description: You made it. The gates close, the walls hold, and you're safe — technically. But the cost is etched into every face. Not everyone who started this journey is standing with you now. Dr. Park tends to the survivors in silence. Rico stares at a name scratched into the wall — a memorial. Zoe keeps her distance from everyone. You survived, but some part of you didn't make it. The dead took that too.

endings.condition: Only the player survived, group-morale collapsed (group-morale below 10)
endings.title: Lone Wolf
endings.description: The safe zone opens its gates to you — just you. The others are gone. Some fell to the dead. Some you lost along the way. Some you had to leave behind. The soldiers ask if anyone else is out there. You shake your head. Inside, people live in groups, families, couples. You sit alone. Safe. Alive. Alone. The apocalypse taught you how to survive — but maybe not how to live.

endings.condition: Critical stats all collapsed (health below 20, supplies below 10, danger-level above 80)
endings.title: The Walking Dead
endings.description: You can see the safe zone lights flickering in the distance, but your legs won't carry you anymore. The bites, the exhaustion, the hunger — it's all catching up. Dr. Park is gone. Rico went down fighting. Zoe disappeared into the shadows like she always said she would. You sit against a tree and watch the lights until they blur. The dead are close. You can hear them. Maybe they were always the lucky ones — they don't have to feel this.

endings.condition: Sacrificed everything for the group (health below 30, group-morale above 70)
endings.title: The Last Stand
endings.description: You gave everything so they could make it. While the others slipped through the drainage pipe to safety, you stayed behind — drawing the horde, buying time, fighting until your blade dulled and your ammo ran out. Rico carries your story through those gates. Dr. Park names the infirmary after you. Zoe carves your name into the wall by the gate. You didn't survive. But they did. And in this world, maybe that's the same thing.

en.name: AI Zombie Survival
en.desc: Lead survivors through the zombie apocalypse! Manage resources, face moral dilemmas, and fight for every breath.
en.description: The dead rose and civilization fell in three days. Now you lead a ragged band of survivors — a cold-blooded medic, a reckless soldier, and a street-smart loner — through 200 miles of zombie-infested wasteland toward a rumored safe zone. Every step is a gamble: ration limited supplies, navigate moral dilemmas that test your humanity, and face the undead hordes that never stop coming. Your choices determine who lives, who dies, and what kind of person you become when the world ends. With AI generating fresh scenarios each playthrough, no two apocalypses are the same.
en.howToPlay: Read each scenario carefully and choose your actions. The stats bar tracks your group's health, supplies, morale, and danger level — every choice affects them. Balance risk with caution: fighting gains ground but costs health, while stealth preserves lives but drains supplies. Characters can die based on your decisions, so choose wisely. There are 5 different endings depending on your group's survival metrics.
tips: Balance aggression and caution — recklessness gets people killed
Keep morale high — a divided group is a dead group
Watch your supplies closely — running out is as deadly as the zombies
Moral dilemmas have no right answer — but your choices shape the ending
Each character has unique strengths — use them wisely

faq.question: Can characters die during the story?
faq.answer: Yes. Your companions can die based on the choices you make. Dr. Park, Rico, and Zoe each have their own survival arcs that depend on your leadership decisions. Some endings have fewer survivors than others.

faq.question: How do the survival stats work?
faq.answer: Four stats track your group's condition: Health (physical wellbeing), Supplies (food, medicine, ammo), Morale (group cohesion and trust), and Danger (zombie threat level). Every choice shifts these stats, and your final stats determine which ending you get.

faq.question: Is this game replayable?
faq.answer: Absolutely! The AI generates fresh scenarios, dialogue, and encounters each time. The core characters and story structure remain, but the specific challenges and moral dilemmas change with every playthrough.

faq.question: How long does one game take?
faq.answer: A typical playthrough takes 15-25 minutes across 5 chapters. Each chapter has 3-6 turns depending on your choices. You can experience all 5 endings by replaying with different strategies.

zh.name: AI 僵尸生存
zh.desc: 带领幸存者穿越僵尸末日！管理资源，面对道德困境，为每一次呼吸而战。
zh.description: 死者复活，文明在三天内崩溃。现在你带领一群衣衫褴褛的幸存者——一个冷酷的医生、一个鲁莽的士兵和一个精明的拾荒者——穿越200英里的僵尸废土，前往一个传说中的安全区。每一步都是赌博：分配有限的补给，面对考验人性的道德困境，对抗永不停止的亡灵大军。你的选择决定谁生、谁死，以及世界末日时你成为什么样的人。AI每次都会生成全新的场景，没有两次末日是相同的。
zh.howToPlay: 仔细阅读每个场景并选择你的行动。状态栏追踪你的团队生命值、补给、士气和危险等级——每个选择都会影响它们。平衡风险与谨慎：战斗能推进进度但消耗生命值，潜行能保全性命但消耗补给。角色可能因你的决定而死亡，所以请慎重选择。根据你的团队生存指标，共有5种不同的结局。
tips: 平衡进攻与谨慎——鲁莽会让人丧命
保持高士气——分裂的团队就是死去的团队
密切关注补给——耗尽补给和僵尸一样致命
道德困境没有正确答案——但你的选择决定结局
每个角色都有独特优势——明智地利用他们

faq.question: 角色会在故事中死亡吗？
faq.answer: 是的。你的同伴可能因你的选择而死亡。朴医生、里科和佐伊各有自己的生存轨迹，取决于你的领导决策。有些结局的幸存者比其他结局少。

faq.question: 生存状态如何运作？
faq.answer: 四个属性追踪你的团队状况：生命值（身体健康）、补给（食物、药品、弹药）、士气（团队凝聚力和信任）和危险等级（僵尸威胁等级）。每个选择都会改变这些属性，最终属性决定你获得哪个结局。

faq.question: 这个游戏可以重玩吗？
faq.answer: 当然可以！AI每次都会生成全新的场景、对话和遭遇。核心角色和故事结构保持不变，但具体的挑战和道德困境在每次游戏中都会变化。

faq.question: 一局游戏需要多长时间？
faq.answer: 通常需要15-25分钟，共5章。根据你的选择，每章有3-6个回合。通过不同的策略重玩可以体验全部5种结局。
